title: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The requested withdrawal is 103,000 ADA, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards at 2% per annum." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork; Provide budget analysis. Residual uncertainty: Treasury diligence dossier incomplete; soft gate applied a -0.1 caution penalty and judged on available repo context (anchor + doctrine + reasoning lean) rather than abstaining. Confidence context: score -0.48; confidence 64.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Reimburse the lost deposit for the Ikigai Info governance action, plus compensation for lost staking rewards.
Recipient: the Ikigai Info governance action submitter
Deliverables: On-chain treasury withdrawal directly to the intended recipient upon enactment
Deadline/expiry: instantly performed as part of the governance action's enactment on-chain
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The Ikigai Info governance action was symbolic and asked the community whether they agreed with a statement thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): A Cardano node bug permitted an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, causing the submitter to be unable to recover a 100,000 ADA deposit.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal states there was significant community sentiment that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed because the submitter was an early pioneer of Cardano governance.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested withdrawal is 103,000 ADA, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards at 2% per annum.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in its budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
missing: Independent evidence for: An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.
Independent evidence for: A Cardano node bug permitted an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, causing the submitter to be unable to recover a 100,000 ADA deposit.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states there was significant community sentiment that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed because the submitter was an early pioneer of Cardano governance.
Independent evidence for: The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in its budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unsustainable (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The requested withdrawal is 103,000 ADA, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards at 2% per annum." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
